Semiconductor

Sourcing the Right MCU: Lessons From The Production Line

July 1, 2026

This article is a draft placeholder — full content coming soon.

A microcontroller that clears every check on paper can still fail once it hits automotive-grade temperature cycling, or an allocation shortage forces a second-source swap. This piece will cover what we actually check beyond the datasheet before recommending an MCU for production.

What we'll cover

  • Temperature cycling and long-term reliability, not just the spec sheet
  • Second-sourcing strategy before you need it, not after an allocation crisis
  • What "automotive-grade" actually guarantees, and what it doesn't
